title: passerina tinctoria var. nivalis
intro: Passerina tinctoria var. nivalis, also known as the Winter Indigo, is a variety of flowering plant in the Borage family, native to North America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent. The plant's health benefits include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Winter Indigo can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
